items.claim: Conor McGregor possesses a significant power advantage over Max Holloway.
Max Holloway lacks one-punch knockout power.
items.quote: Max Holloway, though he is blessed, has never been blessed with like one punch knockout power.

items.claim: Max Holloway has superior cardio and the ability to fight effectively for five rounds compared to Conor McGregor.
items.quote: He's got that long cardio. He's got the ability to fight for five rounds.

items.claim: Conor McGregor's cardio issues were exposed in his fights against Nate Diaz.
items.quote: it's just something we saw Nate Diaz expose in Conor McGregor's game.

items.claim: Max Holloway utilizes a 'float' technique where he pushes off his lead foot to dart back and avoid strikes.
items.quote: you push off that lead foot, you just kind of dart back a little bit. He does that so well.

items.claim: Conor McGregor was exposed for being too flat-footed in his fights against Dustin Poirier.
items.quote: Connor against Porier got exposed for being too flatfooted.

items.claim: Max Holloway is difficult to hit, which requires high-level timing to land punches on him.
items.quote: Max Holloway is super hard to hit. And that's one of the first things that goes is the timing.

items.claim: Conor McGregor's demeanor shifts negatively when he faces resistance and gets hit in a fight.
items.quote: when there is resistance set forth and he starts getting touched... this wilt starts to play in.

items.claim: Moving up to 170 pounds will likely make Conor McGregor slower and negatively impact his cardio.
items.quote: Is he going to have better cardio or worse cardio? Is he going to be faster or slower maybe is the better way to say it. I think probably slower.

items.claim: Max Holloway's speed will be double that of Conor McGregor's in their matchup.
items.quote: I think Max's speed is going to be double Conor McGregor's speed.
